title: Observability, Service Levels, Support, and Incident Learning · Stakeholder Workshop
summary: A guided product team extends user-centered operations and incident system while preserving every earlier problem, accessibility, security, testing, operations, and recovery requirement. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of user-centered operations and incident system while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose user-centered availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, or support indicators with valid measurement boundaries.
Set objective, window, population, exclusions, error budget, and response policy from user need and operating capacity.
Correlate revision, request, actor class, workflow, dependency, event, log, metric, and trace without high-cardinality or privacy leakage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Service Level Indicator
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Many logs report healthy servers while users cannot complete the task and support cannot correlate the failure with actor, workflow, dependency, or revision. The workshop variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Service Level Indicator.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose user-centered availability, correctness, latency, freshness, durability, or support indicators with valid measurement boundaries. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Service Level Indicator, keep Service Level Indicator connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Many logs report healthy servers while users cannot complete the task and support cannot correlate the failure with actor, workflow, dependency, or revision. The workshop variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Service Level Indicator condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: SLSA specification — Controls source and build tracks, provenance, artifact identity, verification, and honest supply-chain claims.

The workshop variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Incident Learning
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for Incident Learning: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Many logs report healthy servers while users cannot complete the task and support cannot correlate the failure with actor, workflow, dependency, or revision. The workshop variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Preserve timeline and evidence, restore safely, communicate impact, analyze contributing conditions without blame, and track preventive work.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop Incident Learning.
steps.why: Retrieving Incident Learning through the concrete user-centered operations and incident system case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Incident Learning, keep Incident Learning connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Preserve timeline and evidence, restore safely, communicate impact, analyze contributing conditions without blame, and track preventive work.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Incident Learning, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Incident Learning precondition, or invariant that must hold.
